name: Activity Feed & Cost Tracking Pages
description: Activity feed full page: chronological event list auto-updating via SSE (useEventSource hook), filterable by agent/project/event type (AC7). Cost & Token dashboard page: per-project and per-agent cost breakdown bar charts, token usage over time line charts (recharts), budget alert configuration stored in SQLite. Gracefully shows 'no data available' placeholder UI when token/cost data is missing from filesystem (AC8). Overview/home dashboard page that aggregates: agent count + status summary cards, active projects count, PACT health snapshot, last 10 activity events, cost burn rate. All monetary values stored as integer cents internally; currency formatting only in display components. Data access through lib/data/ layer only. Server components by default; client components only for SSE feed, chart wrappers, filter controls, and budget alert forms.

types.name: ISOTimestamp
types.description: Branded string type representing an ISO 8601 timestamp (e.g. '2025-01-15T09:30:00.000Z'). All timestamps in the system use this type.

types.name: TimeGranularity
types.description: Granularity for time-series aggregation queries.

types.name: PactHealthStatus
types.description: Health status of the PACT daemon/system.

types.name: DataAvailability
types.description: Status type for empty state rendering. Indicates whether data is present, absent but expected, or not yet available.

types.name: ConnectionStatus
types.description: Status of the SSE connection from the client hook.

types.name: ActivityEventType
types.description: Discriminator literal for all known activity event kinds.

types.name: CostScope
types.description: Scope for cost/token queries — whether to filter by project, agent, or view all.

types.description: How to group cost breakdown entries.

types.name: DataResult
types.description: Discriminated union for all data access returns. Discriminated on the 'ok' boolean field. Components use type narrowing to handle success/error branches.

functions.name: getActivityEvents
functions.description: Retrieves paginated activity events from filesystem sources (OpenClaw sessions, PACT proposals, agent logs). Applies optional filters for agent, project, and event type. Returns cursor-based pagination for infinite scroll. Reads from filesystem via lib/data/ layer — never queries SQLite for event data.

inputs.name: filters
inputs.description: Optional filter criteria. Empty/default filters return all events.

inputs.name: cursor
inputs.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous call. Empty string for first page.

inputs.name: limit
inputs.description: Maximum number of events to return (1-100).

error_cases.name: invalid_cursor
error_cases.condition: Cursor string is malformed or references a position that no longer exists

error_cases.name: filesystem_error
error_cases.condition: Cannot read event source files (OpenClaw sessions, PACT proposals, agent logs)

error_cases.name: parse_error
error_cases.condition: Event source files exist but contain malformed/unparseable data

preconditions: cursor is either empty string (first page) or a valid cursor from a previous getActivityEvents call
If filters.event_types is provided, all values must be valid ActivityEventType variants
postconditions: On success, data contains PaginatedActivityEvents with events.length <= limit
Events are ordered by timestamp descending (newest first)
next_cursor is empty string if and only if there are no more events matching the filters
Each event has a unique, stable id
All event timestamps are valid ISOTimestamp values

structured_side_effects.target: OpenClaw session directories and PACT proposal files
structured_side_effects.description: Reads agent session logs, PACT proposal/vote files from configured filesystem paths.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is total events scanned before cursor + limit

functions.name: getCostTimeSeries
functions.description: Retrieves cost data aggregated into time-series buckets for line/area chart rendering. Reads raw cost data from filesystem and aggregates by the specified granularity. Returns DataResult with DataAvailability semantics — returns empty array with no_data availability when cost files are missing (AC8).

inputs.name: scope
inputs.description: Scope of the query — all costs, or filtered to a specific project/agent.

inputs.name: granularity
inputs.description: Time bucket size for aggregation.

inputs.name: range
inputs.description: Time range to query.

error_cases.name: filesystem_error
error_cases.condition: Cannot read cost data files from OpenClaw directories

error_cases.name: parse_error
error_cases.condition: Cost data files exist but contain malformed data

error_cases.name: invalid_scope
error_cases.condition: scope is 'project' or 'agent' but scope_id does not match any known entity

error_cases.name: invalid_range
error_cases.condition: from_timestamp >= to_timestamp

preconditions: If scope.scope is 'project' or 'agent', scope.scope_id must be non-empty
granularity is a valid TimeGranularity variant
postconditions: On success, data contains CostTimeSeriesPointList ordered by timestamp ascending
All amount_cents values are non-negative integers
Time buckets are contiguous with no gaps (zero-filled for periods with no cost)
First bucket starts at or after range.from_timestamp, last bucket starts before range.to_timestamp

structured_side_effects.description: Reads cost entries from agent session cost files in the OpenClaw directory.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is number of raw cost entries in the range

functions.name: getCostBreakdown
functions.description: Retrieves cost data grouped by project, agent, or model for bar chart rendering. Computes percentage of total for each group. Returns DataResult with proper DataAvailability when cost data is missing from filesystem.

inputs.name: group_by
inputs.description: How to group the breakdown — by project, agent, or model.

inputs.name: range
inputs.description: Time range to query.

error_cases.name: filesystem_error
error_cases.condition: Cannot read cost data files

error_cases.name: parse_error
error_cases.condition: Cost data files contain malformed data

error_cases.name: invalid_range
error_cases.condition: from_timestamp >= to_timestamp

preconditions: group_by is a valid CostBreakdownGroupBy variant
postconditions: On success, data contains CostBreakdownEntryList ordered by total_amount_cents descending
All percentage values sum to approximately 100.0 (within floating point tolerance)
All total_amount_cents values are non-negative
Each entry includes aggregated TokenUsage

structured_side_effects.description: Reads and aggregates cost entries from filesystem.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is number of raw cost entries in the range

functions.name: getTokenTimeSeries
functions.description: Retrieves token usage data aggregated into time-series buckets for line chart rendering. Breaks down into input, output, cache read, and cache write token categories. Gracefully returns empty data with 'no_data' availability when token data files are missing (AC8).

inputs.name: scope
inputs.description: Scope of the query.

inputs.name: granularity
inputs.description: Time bucket size.

inputs.name: range
inputs.description: Time range to query.

error_cases.name: filesystem_error
error_cases.condition: Cannot read token data files

error_cases.name: parse_error
error_cases.condition: Token data files contain malformed data

error_cases.name: invalid_scope
error_cases.condition: scope_id does not match known entity

error_cases.name: invalid_range
error_cases.condition: from_timestamp >= to_timestamp

preconditions: If scope.scope is 'project' or 'agent', scope.scope_id must be non-empty
postconditions: On success, data contains TokenTimeSeriesPointList ordered by timestamp ascending
All token count values are non-negative integers
total_tokens equals sum of input_tokens + output_tokens + cache_read_tokens + cache_write_tokens for each point
Time buckets are contiguous with zero-fill for empty periods

structured_side_effects.description: Reads token usage entries from agent session files.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is number of raw token entries in the range

functions.name: getDashboardSummary
functions.description: Assembles the complete overview dashboard data in a single call to minimize client waterfalls. Reads agent status from OpenClaw, PACT health from PACT CLI/files, recent events from filesystem, and cost burn rate from cost data. Each sub-section degrades gracefully — a failure in one section (e.g., PACT unreachable) does not fail the entire summary.

error_cases.name: total_failure
error_cases.condition: All data sources are inaccessible simultaneously (filesystem unreadable)

error_cases.name: timeout
error_cases.condition: Assembling the summary exceeds the timeout budget

postconditions: On success, data contains DashboardSummary
recent_events contains at most 10 events, ordered newest first
cost_availability reflects actual filesystem data presence
pact_health.status is 'unreachable' if PACT files/CLI are not accessible (not an error)
All integer-cents values in cost_burn_rate are non-negative
fetched_at is a valid ISOTimestamp representing when the summary was assembled

structured_side_effects.target: OpenClaw agent sessions, PACT files, cost data files
structured_side_effects.description: Reads multiple filesystem sources to assemble the dashboard summary.

functions.name: getBudgetAlerts
functions.description: Retrieves all budget alert configurations from SQLite. Returns the full list — budget alerts are expected to be a small set (typically <50).

error_cases.name: db_error
error_cases.condition: SQLite query fails (database locked, corrupted, etc.)

postconditions: On success, data contains BudgetAlertList
Alerts are ordered by created_at descending (newest first)
All threshold_cents values are positive integers
All timestamps are valid ISOTimestamp values

structured_side_effects.description: Reads budget_alerts table from the SQLite database.

functions.name: createBudgetAlert
functions.description: Creates a new budget alert configuration in SQLite. Validates input with Zod schema. Generates a UUID for the new alert and sets created_at/updated_at timestamps.

inputs.name: input
inputs.description: Budget alert configuration to create. Validated with Zod before insertion.

error_cases.name: validation_error
error_cases.condition: Input fails Zod validation (missing fields, invalid values)

error_cases.name: db_error
error_cases.condition: SQLite insert fails

error_cases.name: duplicate_name
error_cases.condition: An alert with the same name already exists for the same scope

preconditions: If input.scope is 'project' or 'agent', input.scope_id must be non-empty
postconditions: On success, data contains the created BudgetAlert with generated id, created_at, and updated_at
The alert exists in the SQLite budget_alerts table
created_at and updated_at are identical on creation

structured_side_effects.description: Inserts a new row into the budget_alerts table.

functions.name: updateBudgetAlert
functions.description: Updates an existing budget alert configuration in SQLite. Validates input with Zod schema. Updates the updated_at timestamp.

inputs.name: id
inputs.description: UUID of the budget alert to update.

inputs.name: input
inputs.description: New configuration values. All fields are applied (full replacement, not partial patch).

error_cases.name: not_found
error_cases.condition: No budget alert exists with the given id

error_cases.name: validation_error
error_cases.condition: Input fails Zod validation

error_cases.name: db_error
error_cases.condition: SQLite update fails

error_cases.name: duplicate_name
error_cases.condition: Another alert (different id) with the same name exists for the same scope

preconditions: A budget alert with the given id exists in the database
postconditions: On success, data contains the updated BudgetAlert
updated_at is set to the current timestamp
created_at is unchanged from the original alert
All other fields reflect the input values

structured_side_effects.description: Updates an existing row in the budget_alerts table.

functions.name: deleteBudgetAlert
functions.description: Deletes a budget alert configuration from SQLite by ID.

inputs.name: id
inputs.description: UUID of the budget alert to delete.

error_cases.name: not_found
error_cases.condition: No budget alert exists with the given id

error_cases.name: db_error
error_cases.condition: SQLite delete fails

postconditions: On success, data contains the deleted BudgetAlert (for undo/confirmation UI)
The alert no longer exists in the SQLite budget_alerts table

structured_side_effects.description: Deletes a row from the budget_alerts table.

functions.name: handleGetEvents
functions.description: API route handler for GET /api/events. Validates query params with Zod EventsQueryParams schema, calls getActivityEvents, returns typed JSON. Returns 400 for validation errors, 500 for internal errors.

inputs.name: query_params
inputs.description: Validated query parameters from the URL.

error_cases.name: validation_error
error_cases.condition: Query params fail Zod schema validation

error_cases.name: internal_error
error_cases.condition: getActivityEvents returns DataResultError

postconditions: Response Content-Type is application/json
HTTP 200 with ApiPaginatedEventsResponse on success
HTTP 400 with ApiErrorResponse on validation failure
HTTP 500 with ApiErrorResponse on internal error

structured_side_effects.description: Delegates to getActivityEvents which reads filesystem.
Logs request params and response status.

functions.name: handleGetEventsStream
functions.description: API route handler for GET /api/events/stream. Establishes an SSE connection with the client. Supports Last-Event-ID header for reconnection. Subscribes to the SSE event bus with optional filters. Sends heartbeat events every 30 seconds to keep the connection alive. Multiplexes all event types over a single stream.

inputs.name: query_params
inputs.description: Validated query/header parameters including optional Last-Event-ID.

error_cases.name: validation_error
error_cases.condition: Query params or headers fail validation

error_cases.name: event_bus_error
error_cases.condition: Cannot subscribe to the SSE event bus

postconditions: Response Content-Type is text/event-stream
Response includes Cache-Control: no-cache
Response includes Connection: keep-alive
Each SSE message includes id, event, and data fields
If Last-Event-ID is provided, missed events since that ID are replayed before live streaming
Heartbeat events are sent every 30 seconds with event type 'heartbeat'
Stream terminates cleanly when client disconnects

structured_side_effects.description: Subscribes to the in-memory SSE event bus for real-time events.
Maintains a long-lived SSE connection to the client.
Logs connection open/close and reconnection events.

functions.name: handleGetCosts
functions.description: API route handler for GET /api/costs. Validates query params with Zod CostQueryParams schema, calls getCostTimeSeries, returns typed JSON with DataAvailability info.

inputs.name: query_params
inputs.description: Validated query parameters.

error_cases.name: validation_error
error_cases.condition: Query params fail Zod validation

error_cases.name: internal_error
error_cases.condition: getCostTimeSeries returns error

postconditions: Response Content-Type is application/json
HTTP 200 on success (even when no data — availability field indicates status)
HTTP 400 on validation failure
HTTP 500 on internal error
Response includes 'availability' field of type DataAvailability

structured_side_effects.description: Delegates to getCostTimeSeries.
Logs request and response.

functions.name: handleGetCostsBreakdown
functions.description: API route handler for GET /api/costs/breakdown. Validates query params, calls getCostBreakdown, returns grouped cost data for bar charts.

inputs.name: query_params
inputs.description: Validated query parameters.

error_cases.name: validation_error
error_cases.condition: Query params fail Zod validation

error_cases.name: internal_error
error_cases.condition: getCostBreakdown returns error

postconditions: Response Content-Type is application/json
HTTP 200 on success with CostBreakdownEntryList
HTTP 400 on validation failure
HTTP 500 on internal error

structured_side_effects.description: Delegates to getCostBreakdown.
Logs request and response.

functions.name: handleGetTokens
functions.description: API route handler for GET /api/tokens. Validates query params, calls getTokenTimeSeries, returns token usage time series.

inputs.name: query_params
inputs.description: Validated query parameters.

error_cases.name: validation_error
error_cases.condition: Query params fail Zod validation

error_cases.name: internal_error
error_cases.condition: getTokenTimeSeries returns error

postconditions: Response Content-Type is application/json
HTTP 200 on success with TokenTimeSeriesPointList and availability
HTTP 400 on validation failure
HTTP 500 on internal error

structured_side_effects.description: Delegates to getTokenTimeSeries.
Logs request and response.

functions.name: handleBudgetAlertsCRUD
functions.description: API route handler for /api/budget-alerts. Dispatches to GET (list all), POST (create), PUT (update by id), DELETE (delete by id). Each operation validates input with Zod and returns typed JSON.

inputs.name: method
inputs.description: HTTP method: GET, POST, PUT, or DELETE.

inputs.name: id
inputs.description: Budget alert ID for PUT/DELETE operations. Empty for GET/POST.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: Request body for POST/PUT operations. Ignored for GET/DELETE.

error_cases.name: method_not_allowed
error_cases.condition: HTTP method is not GET, POST, PUT, or DELETE

error_cases.name: validation_error
error_cases.condition: Body fails Zod validation for POST/PUT

error_cases.name: not_found
error_cases.condition: Alert id not found for PUT/DELETE

error_cases.name: duplicate_name
error_cases.condition: Alert name already exists for same scope

error_cases.name: db_error
error_cases.condition: SQLite operation fails

preconditions: method is one of GET, POST, PUT, DELETE
For PUT and DELETE, id must be a non-empty string
For POST and PUT, body must pass BudgetAlertInput Zod validation
postconditions: GET returns HTTP 200 with BudgetAlertList
POST returns HTTP 201 with created BudgetAlert
PUT returns HTTP 200 with updated BudgetAlert
DELETE returns HTTP 200 with deleted BudgetAlert
All error responses use ApiErrorResponse format

structured_side_effects.description: Reads budget_alerts table.
Creates, updates, or deletes rows in budget_alerts.
Logs all CRUD operations.

functions.name: handleGetDashboardSummary
functions.description: API route handler for GET /api/dashboard/summary. Calls getDashboardSummary and returns the aggregated overview data. No query params needed.

error_cases.name: internal_error
error_cases.condition: getDashboardSummary returns TOTAL_FAILURE or TIMEOUT

postconditions: Response Content-Type is application/json
HTTP 200 with DashboardSummary on success
HTTP 500 with ApiErrorResponse on total failure

structured_side_effects.description: Delegates to getDashboardSummary which reads multiple data sources.
Logs request and response.

functions.name: useEventSource
functions.description: React client hook that establishes an SSE connection to /api/events/stream with optional filters. Returns accumulated events, connection status, and new event count with acknowledge function. Handles automatic reconnection with exponential backoff. Respects Last-Event-ID for gap-free reconnection. This is a 'use client' hook.

inputs.name: filters
inputs.description: Optional filters to apply to the SSE stream. Changes to filters reconnect with new params.

error_cases.name: connection_failed
error_cases.condition: Cannot establish SSE connection (network error, server down)

error_cases.name: parse_error
error_cases.condition: Received SSE event data is not valid JSON

preconditions: Called within a React component tree (client component)
Browser environment with EventSource API support
postconditions: Returns EventSourceState with current connection status
events array is ordered newest-first and capped at 500 items to prevent memory issues
new_count increments for each received event until acknowledge() is called
On unmount, SSE connection is closed and resources are cleaned up
On filter change, old connection is closed and new one is opened

structured_side_effects.description: Maintains a long-lived SSE connection to the server.
Updates events array, status, and new_count state.

functions.name: useCostData
functions.description: React client hook that fetches cost time-series data from /api/costs with SWR-style caching and revalidation. Returns data, loading state, and DataAvailability for empty state rendering. This is a 'use client' hook.

inputs.name: scope
inputs.description: Query scope (all, project, agent).

inputs.name: granularity
inputs.description: Time bucket granularity.

inputs.name: range
inputs.description: Time range for the query.

error_cases.name: fetch_error
error_cases.condition: Network error or API returns 5xx

error_cases.name: validation_error
error_cases.condition: API returns 400 due to invalid params

preconditions: Called within a React component tree (client component)
postconditions: Returns CostDataState reflecting current fetch state
is_loading is true during initial fetch and revalidation
availability is 'no_data' when API returns empty data set (not an error)
availability is 'error' when API call fails
availability is 'available' when data is present
data is empty array (not null) when unavailable — safe to pass to recharts

structured_side_effects.description: Fetches cost data from the API endpoint.
Updates data, loading, and availability state.

functions.name: renderStatCard
functions.description: Pure server component that renders a single stat card for the overview dashboard. Accepts StatCardProps and renders a styled card with title, value, optional trend indicator, and optional icon. Shows EmptyState placeholder when availability is not 'available'. No client-side interactivity needed.

inputs.name: props
inputs.description: Card display properties.

preconditions: props.value is non-empty when availability is 'available'
postconditions: Returns valid React server component JSX (rendered HTML)
When availability is 'no_data' or 'not_configured', renders placeholder instead of value
When trend is provided, renders trend indicator with appropriate color (green for 'down' cost trend, etc.)
Component uses Tailwind CSS classes and CSS variables for styling
Component supports dark mode by default

structured_side_effects.description: Pure render function with no side effects.

functions.name: renderEmptyState
functions.description: Pure server component that renders a placeholder UI for missing/unavailable data (AC8). Used when cost, token, or activity data is not available from the filesystem. Provides contextual messaging and optional action link.

inputs.name: props
inputs.description: Empty state display properties.

postconditions: Returns valid React server component JSX
Renders icon, title, description, and optional action button
Uses muted/subtle styling to indicate absence rather than error
Supports dark mode by default

structured_side_effects.description: Pure render function with no side effects.

functions.name: formatCentsToCurrency
functions.description: Pure utility function that formats integer cents to a USD currency display string. Used only in display/UI layer — never for computation. Handles edge cases like zero, very large values, and negative values (for refunds/adjustments).

inputs.name: amount_cents
inputs.description: Amount in integer cents.

inputs.name: compact
inputs.description: If true, uses compact notation for large values (e.g. '$1.2K' instead of '$1,200.00').

postconditions: Returns a string starting with '$' (or '-$' for negative values)
Non-compact format includes exactly 2 decimal places
Compact format uses K/M suffixes for values >= 1000 cents ($10+)
Zero returns '$0.00' (non-compact) or '$0' (compact)

structured_side_effects.description: Pure function with no side effects.

functions.name: formatTokenCount
functions.description: Pure utility function that formats token counts for display. Uses compact notation (e.g. '1.2M tokens') for large values.

inputs.name: count
inputs.description: Token count.

postconditions: Returns a human-readable string with appropriate suffix (K, M, B)
Values under 1000 are shown as-is
Suffix values include 1 decimal place (e.g. '1.2M')

structured_side_effects.description: Pure function with no side effects.

invariants: All monetary values are stored and computed as integer cents (int); conversion to currency display strings happens only in formatCentsToCurrency at the UI layer
All timestamps throughout the system are ISOTimestamp branded strings in ISO 8601 format with timezone
Components never read files or query SQLite directly — all data access goes through the lib/data/ layer functions defined in this contract

Server components are used by default; client components ('use client') are used only for: ActivityFeed (SSE via useEventSource), chart wrappers (recharts requires client), filter controls (interactivity), and budget alert forms (interactivity)
The SSE endpoint multiplexes all event types over a single stream; clients filter client-side or via query params
All API query parameters are validated with Zod schemas before processing; invalid params return HTTP 400 with ApiErrorResponse
When filesystem data sources are missing or unreadable, functions return success with empty data and DataAvailability='no_data' rather than throwing errors (graceful degradation per AC8)
PACT health check failures result in PactHealthStatus='unreachable', not system errors — the dashboard continues to function
Budget alerts are the only data written to SQLite by this component; all other data is read from the filesystem
The useEventSource hook caps accumulated events at 500 items to prevent unbounded memory growth in the browser
Token counts (input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_read_tokens, cache_write_tokens) are always non-negative integers
CostBreakdownEntry percentage values across all entries for a single query sum to approximately 100.0
Time series responses contain contiguous, zero-filled buckets with no gaps within the requested range
SSE reconnection uses Last-Event-ID to replay missed events, ensuring no event loss during brief disconnections
Zod validation schemas for API params are shared between client (URL construction) and server (param validation) to ensure consistency
The getDashboardSummary function degrades gracefully per sub-section — a failure in PACT health does not prevent agent summary or cost burn rate from being returned
requires: lib/db/ SQLite schema with budget_alerts table
lib/pact/ PACT CLI wrapper + file parser
